dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7462 IH: Farmers’ Assistance and Immediate Disaster Relief Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Carter of Georgia (for himself, Mr. Bishop, and Mr. Allen) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture

form.official-title: To amend the Federal Crop Insurance Act to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to use certain data sets when determining eligibility related to indemnity payments under the Hurricane Insurance Protection-Wind Index endorsement, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Farmers’ Assistance and Immediate Disaster Relief Act or the Farmers’ AID Relief Act.

subsection.text: Section 508 of the Federal Crop Insurance Act (7 U.S.C. 1508) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: Beginning with crop year 2027, in determining whether a county is eligible with respect to a hurricane weather event for an indemnity payment under HIP–WI, the Secretary shall—

clause.text: use the IBTrACS data set; or
subject to subparagraph (B), use the alternative data set compiled pursuant to paragraph (2).

subparagraph.header: Condition for use of alternative data set
subparagraph.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A)(ii), the Secretary may only use the alternative data set compiled pursuant to paragraph (2) if—

clause.text: the data set specified in subparagraph (A)(i) is incomplete, as determined by the Secretary; and
one of the following conditions is met:

subclause.text: A weather station operated by National Weather Service in the county subject to the determination of eligibility in subparagraph (A), or an adjacent county, was damaged.
Any other source of data for making such determination in such county or an adjacent county was damaged.
Any such station or other source provided incomplete data, as determined by the Secretary.

paragraph.text: For purposes of determining eligibility under paragraph (1)(A)(ii), the Secretary shall compile, and make available to the public on the website of the Department related to HIP–WI, an alternative data set for the hurricane weather event prompting such determination using data that—

subparagraph.text: is collected for a specified time period during such weather event, as determined by the Secretary;
contains the same types of data contained in the IBTrACS data set; and
is consistent with the form of such types of data, as determined by the Secretary.
The term HIP–WI means the Hurricane Insurance Protection-Wind Index endorsement established in 2020 by the Risk Management Agency of the Department.

The term IBTrACS data set means the International Best Track Archive for Climate Stewardship data set from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, including the season, name, time, location in latitude and longitude, maximum sustained winds, hurricane category, and wind extents data associated with a hurricane weather event.
The term alternative data set means data collected by a weather station that is—

clause.text: operated by a land-grant college or university (as defined in section 1404 of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 (7 U.S.C. 3103)); and

certified, at the time data is collected for purposes of paragraph (2), as compliant with standards issued by the National Weather Service, including the standards described in the directive issued on November 9, 2023, entitled National Weather Service Instruction 10–1302 (or successor directives).

subsection.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Agriculture shall—

paragraph.text: issue such rules as may be necessary to implement the amendment made by this section; and
revise any guidance relating to the Hurricane Insurance Protection-Wind Index endorsement established in 2020 by the Risk Management Agency of the Department of Agriculture.
